context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=3; meal=strawberries with whipped cream; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=no cue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=no cue; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or a cold dessert AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context includes watching TV in front, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to heat strawberries with whipped cream, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking a social partner on the right. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user chose to preserve visibility toward a right-side attentional target. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to keep the front visual field clear while attending to a front-facing TV.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected a quicker interaction style in that attention-sharing context. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort transfer style that reduces the need to lean forward while attending to a right-side visual target. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again chose direct delivery while engaged with a front-facing visual target, indicating a broader TV/attention-sharing preference for lower-effort transfer.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected an outside-mouth transfer that balances comfort and reachability in a social setting. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when the robot delivers food directly into the mouth.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user chose to remove readiness notifications in a front-TV context, consistent with preferring less interruption and more streamlined automation while visually engaged. No broader context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling, which is consistent with avoiding ambiguity from conversational mouth movements in social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit initiation, indicating a stable tendency toward deliberate user control in that context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected fully automatic initiation in that context, suggesting that when attention is directed to a front-facing TV, reduced interaction burden takes priority over explicit control.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling for drink initiation in a social setting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected explicit initiation for drinking. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation for drinking while engaged with a front-facing TV, indicating a lower-effort interaction style in that context.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling for mouth-wiping initiation in a social setting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user again selected explicit initiation for wiping. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation for wiping in that context, again favoring reduced effort while attention is occupied by a front-facing TV.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user chose to suppress completion notifications in that context, consistent with preferring minimal interruption and a more seamless interaction flow while watching TV.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user preferred reliable automatic detection for bite completion rather than requiring extra manual input. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user again chose automatic bite-completion detection. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user continued to prefer reliable automatic bite-completion detection, indicating stable trust in automation for feeding completion across observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit completion signaling for drinking. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit completion signaling for drinking. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion for drinking in that context, indicating that reduced effort can override explicit control when attention is occupied by a front-facing TV.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit completion signaling for mouth wiping. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit completion signaling for wiping. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion for wiping in that context, again favoring lower-effort interaction while watching TV.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in a social setting, even at the cost of longer meal time. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user also preferred retracting when a right-side visual target was present. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to retract, indicating a preference for faster, less interrupted interaction in that front-TV context despite potential obstruction concerns.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred some dipping while limiting messiness in a social context. IF food has no dippable item or sauce, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is food without sauce or dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed meal had no dipping component and the user selected no dipping. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is fruit with topping but no dippable sauce, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for strawberries with whipped cream, consistent with treating that meal as non-dippable.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait before automatic continuation, consistent with wanting ample time while also focusing on a right-side visual activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait, indicating a preference for rapid automatic progression and low interaction overhead in that context.
